content.problemStatement:
Guddu was participating in a programming contest. He only had one problem left when his mother called him for dinner. Guddu is well aware how angry his mother could get if he was late for dinner and he did not want to sleep on an empty stomach, so he had to leave that last problem to you. Can you solve it on his behalf?

For a given sequence of positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, you are supposed to find the number of triples (i,j,k)(i, j, k)(i,j,k) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§k‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le k \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§k‚Č§N and

Ai‚äēAi+1‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAj‚ąí1=Aj‚äēAj+1‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAk‚ÄČ,A_i \oplus A_{i+1} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_{j-1} = A_j \oplus A_{j+1} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_k \,,Ai‚Äč‚äēAi+1‚Äč‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAj‚ąí1‚Äč=Aj‚Äč‚äēAj+1‚Äč‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAk‚Äč,

where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of triples.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The triples are (1,3,3)(1, 3, 3)(1,3,3), since 5‚äē2=75 \oplus 2 = 75‚äē2=7, and (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), since 5=2‚äē75 = 2 \oplus 75=2‚äē7.
